title: Object 56
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Bottled Starlight‌}}
{{Psychedelic Expansion |id=1 |content='''Starlight, only ever shines in the darkest nights.‌''' |size=15px }}

'''Bottled Starlight'''‌, one of the rarest resources in the Backrooms, is a treasure that countless wanderers have dreamed of but cannot obtain. It is not an artificial creation, nor can it be replicated by any known means, and it only forms naturally under specific conditions, which means each ‌'''Bottled Starlight'''‌ is completely unique.

==Appearance and Properties‌==

Under normal circumstances, '''‌Bottled Starlight'''‌ appears as a brand-new glass jar with varying sizes and shapes from case to case. The body of the glass jar is smooth with almost no visible cracks, and there is no fixed form for its shape, the common shapes recorded include Mason jars, wide-mouth jars, as well as uniquely shaped wishing bottles and drift bottles; the stoppers are mostly corks or screw caps with excellent sealing performance. In addition, the bottle body will occasionally be tied with ribbons, hanging ornaments or handwritten paper labels, and these decorations also vary from bottle to bottle.

{{Blurimage |file=浩瀚星辰.jpeg |width=275px |caption={{Text Transition|id=1|before=''The vast expanse of the Milky Way stretches brilliantly, as if rising from within its depths.''|after=The most well-known image of Bottled Starlight.‌|time=10s}} }}

As mentioned above, the outer wall of ‌'''Bottled Starlight'''‌ is made of pure and impurity-free glass, with a thickness ranging from 0.5 centimeters to 3 centimeters, and has excellent light transmittance. However, this glass is extremely brittle and cannot withstand bumps or falls. Even a slight impact can easily shatter and destroy it, and the starlight inside will dissipate completely along with it. This fragility makes the preservation of this item even more difficult, which also means that the existence of every ‌'''Bottled Starlight'''‌ is even more precious.

What is truly amazing is the scene inside ‌'''Bottled Starlight'''. Through the transparent outer glass wall, a real, vast night sky will appear before your eyes. Countless stars twinkle in the deep indigo firmament—some are distant, faint points of light, while others are bright starlight with halos; the hazy Milky Way slowly moves and flows along with the scattered starlight, constructing a quiet, secluded world.

Beneath the star curtain‌, wanderers can always find a projection of the scenery of the level they are currently in. In other words, the space below the flowing star river inside the bottle will faintly reflect the unique atmosphere and outline of the level where the holder of ‌'''Bottled Starlight'''‌ is located. Current academia generally believes that ‌'''Bottled Starlight‌''' can sense the surrounding environment through unknown methods, and display the surrounding environment inside the bottle.

In addition, it has been observed that the glass body of ‌'''Bottled Starlight'''‌ also seems to have a special optical effect: it will change the appearance of the internal starlight according to the light changes of the surrounding environment. In the dark, the starlight inside the bottle will become brighter enough to illuminate a small surrounding area; while in places with stronger light, the starlight will become softer, adding a hazy aesthetic beauty.

If the stopper of '''Bottled Starlight''' is opened, wanderers will see the entire vast sea of stars slowly drifting out from the bottle's mouth, with flowing light overflowing and stardust scattering, and everything around will be instantly enveloped by boundless night and hazy starlight. The Milky Way, originally confined within the bottle, will no longer be restrained by the glass, slowly unfolding and flowing in mid-air. Meanwhile, a crisp and gentle night breeze will carry the unique serene atmosphere of [[The Frontrooms|the Frontrooms']] night sky, rushing towards one's face. However, if the stopper is not replaced for a long time, and the starlight inside dissipates beyond a certain extent, '''Bottled Starlight''' will gradually lose its luster, and the entire overflowed starry sky will become dim, never to be restored to its original state; at this point, '''Bottled Starlight''' is no different from an ordinary glass bottle.

Research conclusions indicate that the starlight contained within the glass of '''Bottled Starlight''' is completely free from the constraints of all known physical rules of the Backrooms and even [[The Frontrooms|the Frontrooms]]. The aforementioned starlight is completely intangible, has an extremely low density, and possesses strong diffusion and self-repair capabilities. This perfectly explains why, once the stopper is pulled out, the vast star curtain sealed within will spread outwards without restraint; and why, even after opening the stopper several times, the starry scene inside the bottle always remains intact and brilliant.

There are reports that the appearance of '''Bottled Starlight''' also changes with the holder's inner state. When the holder is calm, the starlight inside the bottle becomes clearer and brighter, the entire night sky feels light and soft, providing warm comfort to wanderers; but once the holder frequently falls into extreme psychological states such as despair, madness, or greed, or is long submerged in such extreme mental states, the starry sky inside the bottle will rapidly distort, the starlight turns scarlet and twisted, the Milky Way becomes turbid and chaotic, and the originally quiet star curtain will gradually transform into shadows of chaos, moreover, this chaotic state will continue to spread, even affecting the psychology of surrounding wanderers, thereby causing panic.

==Usage and Value‌==

As is well known, in the Backrooms, '''Bottled Starlight''' possesses extremely high practicality and unique value; correct usage will inevitably be of great benefit to wanderers. Meanwhile, due to the extremely rare and difficult acquisition channels of '''Bottled Starlight''', as well as its well-deserved practicality, the actual value of this item has surpassed the vast majority of items in the Backrooms; in formal transactions, a bottle of intact '''Bottled Starlight''' can usually be exchanged for hundreds of bottles of [[Object 1|Almond Water]], and can even be equivalent to various top-tier medical equipment or weaponry.

{{Blurimage |file=星河欲转千帆舞.jpeg |width=275px |caption={{Text Transition|id=2|before=''The Milky Way seems to turn, a thousand sails dance on the waves.''|after=The image shows the appearance of two bottles of Bottled Starlight when they were first discovered.|time=10s}} }}

Firstly, the most basic and widely applied usage of '''Bottled Starlight''' is to serve as a vessel for spiritual comfort. The breathtaking scenery within this item is originally prepared for wanderers as the ultimate spiritual enjoyment and psychological healing. When gazing at the beautiful starry sky inside the bottle, wanderers will involuntarily relax their body and mind, forget the fear and pressure from the outside world, and obtain brief tranquility. This kind of spiritual satisfaction is a precious wealth that no supplies in the Backrooms can replace.

In dark and lightless environments, '''Bottled Starlight''' can also serve as temporary lighting. In the darkness, '''Bottled Starlight''' will release a brighter glow, illuminating the surrounding environment, while not being enough to attract the attention of entities, thereby helping wanderers explore the level.

The nutritional value of '''Bottled Starlight''' is equally worthy of its name. Drinking the galaxy flowing in the bottle can effectively soothe the restlessness, gloom, and despair in the wanderer's heart, allowing one to obtain long-lasting peace, breeding rare joy and relaxation, while the clear and gentle power will quickly penetrate the whole body. Furthermore, the starlight can also cure diseases suffered by the consumer to a certain extent, and quickly restore exhausted physical strength and spirit.

However, due to the extreme scarcity and high value of '''Bottled Starlight''', wanderers holding this item are very likely to be targeted by other wanderers. In chaotic trading levels, violent robberies for a bottle of '''Bottled Starlight''' are common. If one does not have sufficient self-protection ability, it is recommended that wanderers carry it discreetly after obtaining Bottled Starlight, avoid public showing off, so as not to become the target of public criticism and fall into fatal crisis.

==Spawn Locations‌==

It is reported that '''Bottled Starlight''' often appears in quiet and dim corners of various levels, usually in hidden spaces rarely visited by entities. The following lists the locations in the Backrooms where '''Bottled Starlight''' has been recorded multiple times, compiled by [[Major Explorer Group]].

'''''Bottled Starlight Discovery Archives: Recorded Common Discovery Locations'''''

'''''Multiple Discoveries'''''
'''''(2 ≤ Number of Discoveries ≤ 3)'''''

> Corners of [[Level 4]];
> Inside buildings in [[Level 9]];
> Inside lockers in [[Level 43]];
> Under streetlights in [[Level 49]];
> Beside houses in [[Level 161]];
> Inside cabins in [[Level 410]];
> Under roller coaster tracks in [[Level 995]].
'''''Frequent Discoveries'''''
'''''(Number of Discoveries ≥ 4)'''''

> In sand piles in [[Level 100]];
> By the roadside in [[Level 150]];
> Beside seats in [[Level 154]];
> On the ground in [[Level 513]];
> On tabletops in [[Level 514]];
> In swimming pools in [[Level 699]];
> Behind flower pots in [[Level 798]].
==Related Organizations‌==

Below are two organizations in the Backrooms associated with '''Bottled Starlight'''.

'''''Major Explorer Group'''''

> Focuses on the exploration, documentation, collection and safety protection of '''Bottled Starlight''';
> Establishes discovery archives of '''Bottled Starlight''', recording the process of t he discovery of each '''Bottled Starlight''';
> Formulates regulations related to '''Bottled Starlight''' and handles relevant incidents in accordance with the regulations;
> Buys back or sells '''Bottled Starlight''' at high prices;
> One of the initiators of the '''Star Curtain Project'''.
'''''Star Worshippers‌'''''

> Worship '''Bottled Starlight''' as a spiritual faith, firmly believing it is the light of hope for the Backrooms;
> Obsessed with collecting and guarding '''Bottled Starlight''', and vigorously promotes the development of various related activities;
> Builds storage warehouses for '''Bottled Starlight''', and continuously expands the inventory through various channels;
> Buys back or sells '''Bottled Starlight''' at high prices;
> One of the initiators of the '''Star Curtain Project'''.
